articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 82% of landlords are worried about the forthcoming Renters’ Rights Act, with nearly half (44%) expressing significant concern.  
- Three-quarters of landlords say increased regulation has deterred them from expanding their portfolios, and almost half plan to sell or downsize within two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Tenants are increasingly prioritizing energy efficiency, prompting landlords to enhance EPC ratings to attract renters ahead of the 2030 mandate for a minimum C rating.
- The surge in energy costs, exacerbated by the Iran War, has intensified renters’ focus on properties that can help them save money on utility bills.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe that planning reforms will benefit their operations, indicating widespread skepticism in the property market.  
- Landbay's findings highlight a significant lack of confidence among landlords regarding the effectiveness of current planning initiatives.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in properties come from external sources, highlighting the need for landlords to prioritize outdoor pipework maintenance.  
- Neglecting exterior plumbing could lead to costly repairs, as experts stress the importance of thorough leak detection and management.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act takes effect on May 1, 2026, bringing significant changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales.  
- Landlords must prepare for new requirements and regulatory oversight designed to equalize landlord and tenant rights.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act, set to take effect on May 1.  
- Alarmingly, 58% of these landlords face a high risk of non-compliance with the new regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are prioritizing portfolio restructuring and financing adjustments over expansion due to shifting rental demand.
- The ONS highlights a slowdown in rental growth, prompting a reassessment of property strategies to ensure alignment with current market conditions.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- The findings highlight growing concerns over accessibility and affordability for new homeowners.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the vital role of the private rented sector (PRS) with a tailored strategy.
- There is growing concern over the potential exodus of landlords, highlighting the need for government support in the PRS.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- New HMRC regulations require tax advisors, including conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, to register by May or risk hefty fines.
- Non-compliance could lead to penalties of up to £10,000, putting landlords and associated professionals on high alert.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that property licensing won't result in increased rents for tenants, despite imposing a £694 per property fee on landlords.  
- Landlords face the financial burden of licensing costs with no expectation of reimbursement, raising concerns about potential impacts on rental affordability.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour party spokesperson Matthew Pennycook firmly states that there will be no implementation of rent controls under their policy.  
- This announcement underscores the party's stance on housing market regulation amidst ongoing debates about affordability.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to regulate the Housing in Multiple Occupation (HMO) market to curb unregulated landlord practices.  
- Detailed guidelines for HMO management are in the works to ensure better oversight and standards in the sector.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A new week-long campaign launches on Monday to educate tenants on their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- Tenants are encouraged to take advantage of the act's provisions to enhance their rental experience.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, as anticipated by property expert Paul Shamplina.
- The surge in evictions highlights concerns over the implications of the new legislation on tenants and landlords alike.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rent prices are on the rise as landlords anticipate the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to new data from Hamptons.  
- This trend highlights a proactive adjustment in the rental market as regulations shift to prioritize tenant protections.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to pass in just 10 days, is poised to eliminate the presence of hobbyist landlords in the UK housing market.  
- The legislation aims to strengthen tenant protections, significantly impacting the landscape for casual property investors.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly turning to property investment, navigating challenges like red tape and heightened tax burdens.  
- This demographic's interest reflects a shift in investment strategies, aiming to build wealth through real estate despite regulatory hurdles.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing significant challenges in the current market, leading to price cuts and increased instances of failed sales, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.  
- Many sellers are opting to withdraw from the market altogether due to these tough conditions.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations
articles.summary:
Laura Walkley, of TWM Solicitors, says HMRC is clamping down on inheritance tax avoidance through estate assessments.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC intensifies oversight of property valuations to tackle inheritance tax avoidance.
- Legal expert Laura Walkley highlights increased scrutiny on estate assessments by tax authorities.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A £135k terrace property in Penrith, Cumbria, drew attention after a tenant was accidentally captured asleep in bed during listing photos and videos.
- The unusual oversight sparked interest and conversation in the real estate market, highlighting the potential pitfalls of property photography.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Referral fees are described as an "unseen surcharge" or "hidden tax" that impacts homebuyers, as noted by the Conveyancing Task Force.  
- This additional cost raises concerns about transparency and affordability in the property market.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond expands its reach into Scotland by entrusting Clyde Property Group with its portfolio in the West.  
- David Alexander highlights the strategic move to strengthen operations north of the border.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue, attributed to geopolitical challenges impacting buyer activity.
- CEO Guy Gittins highlights a significant decline in new buyer engagement, underscoring tough market conditions.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation is climbing sharply, predominantly affecting fuel and transport costs due to the ongoing Middle East conflict.
- Experts warn that the price increases are likely to ripple through the broader economy in the near future.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, reveals that 90% of new homes never make it to the open sales market.  
- This trend highlights a significant disconnect in the housing market, potentially limiting options for buyers.
